preamble: The smoothing of the diphthong [oi] to a nasalized vowel [õ] involves the loss of the glide and the stabilization of the nasalized monophthong.

examples.note: Historical stage involving diphthongal intermediates that simplified to nasalized vowels.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
